read: Source inspection remains correct: SparseLightningIndexerGradKLLoss does not consume SparseAttnSharedkvGrad dQ/dKV/d_sinks outputs and can be scheduled independently in principle. The direct same-process MI350 measurement shows the naive independent-stream schedule is not a materialized latency promotion: LI/KL is 4.34 ms alone but stretches to about 16.14 ms when overlapped with sparse-attention branches, so stream contention erases most of the theoretical lower bound. Keep the dependency split as a scheduling fact, but do not promote this two-stream/four-branch stopgap; the main sparse path still needs a fused source-boundary sparse-attention backward with group-aware compressed-dKV epilogue, and LI/KL needs either separate resource-aware scheduling or fusion only where it does not contend with the same QK/P/dP replay resources.
